headline: Chips Hold the Line as Broad Market Buckles Under Geopolitical Weight
body_html:
Monday's session ended with clear fault lines drawn across the tape. Of the 501 names in our universe, just 146 finished higher against 352 that closed in the red — a lopsided breadth reading that underscored the session's defensive posture. Semiconductors were the lone sector to post a gain, edging up 0.46%, while every other group surrendered ground, with Homebuilders leading the retreat at a loss of 1.82% and Industrials, Energy, and Healthcare each dropping more than 1%.

The geopolitical backdrop kept risk appetite in check throughout the day. Reports of fresh US troop casualties over the weekend, a vow of retaliation against Iran, a Houthi naval blockade declared against Saudi Arabia, and tanker attacks off Oman created a persistent ceiling on conviction. Oil prices, notably, refused to spike despite the drumbeat of headlines — a dynamic traders and analysts alike found difficult to explain away.

The standout mover of the session was e.l.f. Beauty, which surged 8.09% — an outlier in an otherwise defensive Consumer sector that fell 0.70% on the day. Meanwhile, chip-adjacent names including Credo Technology and MaxLinear added better than 4% each, confirming that AI-infrastructure enthusiasm, amplified by fresh news on a major tech company's chip roadmap, remains the market's most reliable anchor when broader sentiment wavers.

briefs.headline: e.l.f. Beauty Dazzles in a Down Tape
briefs.body: Consumer-cosmetics name e.l.f. Beauty posted the session's biggest gain, advancing 8.09% on Monday. The move came against a backdrop of broad Consumer-sector weakness, making the outperformance all the more striking. No single catalyst was immediately apparent, though momentum traders took notice of the decisive break higher.

briefs.headline: Semiconductor Pocket Keeps Indices From Deeper Losses
briefs.body: Semiconductors were the only sector to finish in positive territory Monday, rising 0.46% as connectivity and test-equipment names added meaningful ground. Credo Technology gained 4.63% and Teradyne climbed 3.54%, suggesting that enthusiasm tied to an AI chip roadmap announcement continued to support the group even as the broader market sold off.

briefs.headline: Energy Names Bruised Despite Muted Crude Reaction
briefs.body: Energy stocks fell 1.05% Monday, with Magnolia Oil & Gas dropping 6.35% among the session's sharpest decliners. The losses arrived even as oil prices remained relatively contained despite a string of escalatory geopolitical events — including tanker attacks near Oman and a Houthi naval blockade — a disconnect that has puzzled market participants for months.

briefs.headline: Solar and Fuel-Cell Shares Hammered on Risk-Off Tone
briefs.body: Clean-energy names bore some of the session's heaviest selling pressure. SolarEdge fell 9.09% and Bloom Energy lost 8.33%, each landing among the top six decliners across the entire universe. The moves reflected a broader rotation away from long-duration, capital-intensive names as geopolitical uncertainty stiffened the discount rate investors mentally apply to future cash flows.

briefs.headline: STAAR Surgical Leads Healthcare Lower
briefs.body: STAAR Surgical was Monday's single worst performer, tumbling 9.34% and dragging an already-struggling Healthcare sector — down 1.03% on the day — further into the red. Inspire Medical Systems offered a rare counterpoint, rising 5.28% to rank as the second-largest gainer in the universe, illustrating the wide dispersion within the device subsector.

briefs.headline: Geopolitical Crosscurrents Dominate the Week's Opening
briefs.body: Monday's macro backdrop featured a US vow of retaliation following troop casualties, a declared Houthi naval blockade against Saudi Arabia, and two tanker vessels reported attacked off Oman. Gulf equity markets showed modest gains on hopes for US-Iran negotiations, but stateside investors appeared unwilling to look past near-term risk, keeping breadth decisively negative at the close.
